summary: Russian forces threatened to launch Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missiles from Belgorod. Ukrainian forces hit a Russian Black Sea Fleet storage site in Sevastopol with a Neptune missile. Multiple Shahed and UAV attacks were reported across Poltava, Zaporizhzhia, and the northern regions.
key_points: Russian threat to fire Iskander‑M/S‑300 ballistic missiles from Belgorod region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546).
Ukrainian Neptune missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet storage site in Sevastopol, destroying weapons and equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Kharkiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/64884, @kpszsu/64871).
Shahed‑type drones repeatedly targeted Poltava, with reports of up to 15 Shaheds inbound (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67572).
Ukrainian drone production workshop in the Chuguev area was destroyed by a Russian Geran drone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Numerous UAV groups were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv and southern Odesa regions (Source: @kpszsu/64883, @kpszsu/64858).
German‑proposed €6.6 billion transfer from the European Peace Fund to Ukraine is blocked by Poland (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30828).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces threatened to launch Iskander‑M/S‑300 missiles from Belgorod. Ukrainian Neptune missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet storage site in Sevastopol. Shahed drones continue to target Poltava and other regions, indicating heightened aerial activity.

points: A Ukrainian Neptune cruise missile hit a Russian Black Sea Fleet storage facility in the Sevastopol bay, destroying ammunition depots and military equipment (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone production workshop in the Chuguev area, impacting UAV manufacturing capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukraine's export capacity is at risk as attacks on Odesa port infrastructure threaten iron ore and agricultural shipments, with companies lacking funds for repairs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30827).
German proposal to transfer €6.6 billion from the European Peace Fund to Ukraine faces blockage by Poland, affecting financing for defense projects (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30828).
Ukrainian authorities reported a 19‑year‑old suspect detained for allegedly poisoning a soldier with methadone sourced from Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30848).
Russian forces announced a threat to launch Iskander‑M and S‑300 ballistic missiles from Belgorod region, raising regional air defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546).
Multiple Shahed‑type drones were reported over Zaporizhzhia, including three Shaheds and two KABs targeting the region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67566).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts, indicating intensified air‑ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/64884, @kpszsu/64871).
A fire erupted near a thermal power plant in Simferopol, Crimea, following an explosion, suggesting possible sabotage or combat damage (Source: @eRadarrua/96289).
Russian forces reported a Shahed‑4 missile heading toward Kropyvnytskyi, adding to the missile threat landscape (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67566).
Shahed drones repeatedly targeted Poltava, with reports of 1, 3, 5, and up to 15 Shaheds inbound from various directions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67553, @mon1tor_ua/67564, @mon1tor_ua/67572).
Guided aviation bombs were dropped by Russian tactical aviation on Donetsk region, indicating active air strikes on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/64884).
UAV groups were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv and southern Odesa, potentially to support operations in the east (Source: @kpszsu/64883).
A Russian Shahed‑4 missile was reported heading toward Kropyvnytskyi, a city near the eastern operational axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67566).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone production workshop destroyed in the Chuguev area, affecting eastern UAV capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Multiple UAV groups were detected over Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with courses toward Chernihiv and northern Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/64830, @kpszsu/64832, @kpszsu/64864).
Reactive Shahed drones were reported heading toward Kyiv region settlements such as Yagotyn/Berezan (Source: @kpszsu/64861).
UAV activity was noted over the northern part of Sumy heading toward Chernihiv, indicating sustained aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/64830).
A group of UAVs from the Black Sea was observed moving northward toward Mykolaiv, potentially crossing northern operational zones (Source: @kpszsu/64883).
UAVs were reported over the northern part of Chernihiv oblast near Snovsk, heading southward (Source: @kpszsu/64864).
Ukrainian Neptune missile strike on Sevastopol destroyed Russian Black Sea Fleet storage sites (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
UAVs from the Black Sea were reported heading toward Mykolaiv and southern Odesa, increasing pressure on southern coastal areas (Source: @kpszsu/64858, @kpszsu/64850).
Fifteen Shahed drones were observed in the Black Sea area moving toward the Southern‑Black Sea sector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).
A fire near a thermal power plant in Simferopol, Crimea, followed an explosion, indicating possible combat activity in the south (Source: @eRadarrua/96289).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv region, affecting southern operational depth (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts, showing increased air strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/64884, @kpszsu/64871).
Ukrainian forces employed a Neptune cruise missile against a Russian naval target in Sevastopol, demonstrating long‑range strike capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81849).
Multiple Shahed and reactive UAV attacks were reported across Poltava, Zaporizhzhia, and northern regions, indicating heavy drone usage by both sides (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67566, @mon1tor_ua/67572).
Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone production workshop in Chuguev, highlighting the use of loitering munitions in the air war (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
UAV groups from the Black Sea moved toward Mykolaiv and Odesa, suggesting coordinated maritime‑air operations (Source: @kpszsu/64883, @kpszsu/64858).

key_developments: Russian forces threatened to launch Iskander‑M/S‑300 ballistic missiles from Belgorod region, raising regional air defence alerts. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546)
Ukrainian Neptune cruise missile struck a Russian Black Sea Fleet storage site in Sevastopol, destroying ammunition and equipment. (Source: @UkraineNow/81849)
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts, indicating intensified air‑ground operations. (Source: @kpszsu/64884, @kpszsu/64871)
Multiple Shahed drones repeatedly targeted Poltava, with reports of up to fifteen inbound, stressing local air defence. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67572)
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone production workshop in the Chuguev area, impacting UAV manufacturing. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837)
UAV groups from the Black Sea moved toward Mykolaiv and southern Odesa, increasing pressure on southern coastal regions. (Source: @kpszsu/64883, @kpszsu/64858)
German‑proposed €6.6 billion transfer from the European Peace Fund to Ukraine is blocked by Poland, affecting defence financing. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30828)
sections.threat_assessment: The imminent threat of Iskander‑M/S‑300 missile launches from Belgorod elevates the risk of high‑payload strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure. Shahed drone swarms continue to overwhelm air defences in central and northern zones, while Russian guided aviation bombings demonstrate sustained air superiority attempts. Ukrainian long‑range capabilities, exemplified by the Neptune strike on Sevastopol, show a growing ability to hit strategic Russian assets, potentially prompting retaliatory escalations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defence networks around Poltava and the northern oblasts, deploying additional missile interceptors to counter Shahed influxes. Southern coastal commands are bolstering coastal artillery and UAV interception assets in response to Black Sea drone incursions. Internationally, financing delays, such as Poland's block of EU funds, may constrain Ukraine's ability to sustain heightened defensive operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Belgorod launch sites is critical for early warning of Iskander‑M activity. UAV traffic from the Black Sea suggests coordinated maritime‑air operations that could target logistics hubs in Mykolaiv and Odesa. Continued assessment of Russian tactical aviation patterns will inform counter‑air strategies, while the destruction of Ukrainian drone workshops may degrade future UAV sortie rates.
